title: Old town the place to be in Quilto
text: Old Town in Quilto is amazing to walk around and enjoy, very much like some of the European on town with the narrow street with cobblestones.Even more impressive when you consider the slopes of streets being in Andes very hilly.All areas in old town are within walking distance so it’s just great to walk around and get lost in the streets, nice variety of shops, restaurants, cafes to enjoy.

title: Very easily explorable on foot.
text: We wondered through the old city of Quito on foot. - exploring, taking it in and enjoying. We, of course, visited the old square, several churches, boutiques and stores. We enjoyed delicious coffee in cafes- like bodega di Cantuna. Several monuments drew our attention.It is a delightful colonial old city easily walkable and navigable.

title: Travel Responsibly
text: Quito: where the sky touches the earth.Quito: where the sky touches the earth.The best of Ecuador to travel and get to know all the architecture and how modern and ancient the city is

title: A very interesting old town
text: We spent three days walking in Quito, and the city really seduced us. The historic centre and the old town are a real colonial jewel, with cobblestone streets, lively squares and colourful facades. Quito, founded in 1534 on the remains of ancient Inca cities, is full of history and architecture: baroque churches such as the Compañía or San Francisco are incredible, often richly decorated and very photogenic. Local markets bring a lively and authentic touch, with crafts, fruit, and local specialties. Every corner offers a discovery, and walking allows you to feel the unique atmosphere of this city that combines heritage, culture and daily life. A real favorite for history and architecture lovers!

title: Historic Center
text: We really enjoyed walking around the Quito main square and other parts of the historic center.  There were some nice spots for photos and it was not particularly crowded.  There were a few vendors including those with tables set up to sell hand crafts as well as some stands selling various candies.  I do recommend going with a guide so that you can gain an understanding.  Overall, a very good experience.

title: Definetely a must when in Quito!
text: Quito Old Town is beautiful, there are so many different churches you can visit and most of them offer you guided tours aswell. I reccomend taking cash as most places do not accept card.

title: Fascinating Old Town, Be Alert After Sundown
text: I really liked Quito's old town. During the day, the streets felt alive with people and daily activities. Stores and restaurants were busy. The old town was interesting and atmospheric.After sunset, the activity ebbed; though, eateries were still open. I felt safe on the main streets; but, some of the dimly lit alleyways looked empty and ominous.

title: Very well preserved.
text: The historic center of Quito is really well preserved, with large squares and many churches, first of all the Basilica and the church of the Society of Jesus. But there are also many hidden gems such as the Bolivar theater and calle la Ronda to give an example.A note of merit to the craft beer that you can find in the convent of San Francisco and the palace of the archbishopric that houses a famous restaurant and a curious history.A note of discord is only for the lack of security with which one enters the high parts of some churches, really dangerous.

title: Please be aware of thieves.
text: My wife and I had one day to spend in Quito on our way to the Galapagos. We stayed on the edge of the Old Town and on Sunday morning walked a seemingly safe and obvious route from Plaza de San Francisco to Independence Square and on up to the Basilica of the National Vow. Sunny day, lots of people about but as we walked up Venezuela to the Basilica, it seemed a bird poohed on my wife from above. A seemingly helpful man carrying a water bottle stared to pour water over some of the ‘poop’. An older man seemingly just walking up the road too pointed upwards to indicate this must have been a bird. It wasn’t. They had thrown a mucky bird-poop like mixture onto my wife and while we were distracted stole the rucksack I had stupidly put on the ground against a wall - they actually swapped an empty black  rucksack for ours which meant it took us a little longer to realise we’d been robbed. We are experienced travellers and thought we were being very careful - beware of the speed and ingenuity of Quito thieves.

title: Private Guide of Quito
text: Spent a full day in Quito before we head to the Galapagos tomorrow for our Lindblad Expeditions National Geographic Expeditions cruise. Through the excellent advice and recommendations from the group we hired Ricardo Villalobos as our guide for the day. He picked us up at the Swissotel (also highly recommend) at 8:45 and for almost 9 hours took us to all the top sites on our list: the middle of the earth/equator, the Church of the Society of Jesus (golden church), Independence Square, lunch at La Vid ( fantastic!), Virgin of the Panecillo on the hill with spectacular views, chocolate shopping, La Ronda ( would only go there with a guide), and shopping at the indoor markets. Ricardo is extremely professional, knowledgeable, accommodating, seriously knows his way around the city, and went above and beyond to cover everything we wanted to see. I felt COMPLETELY safe with him and would not tour the city on my own. Traffic is crazy and neighborhoods can change quickly. His car is super clean and comfortable. And he’s extremely reasonable. He has excellent reviews on Trip Advisor which are well earned. I highly highly recommend him! Lastly, every single Ecuadorian we met was friendly, gracious and welcoming. They are lovely people!”

title: Not accessible!!!
text: We went to old town on Saturday January 5th and most of the main tourist streets were completely barricaded off. About 3 blocks in every direction Plaza Grande was blocked off by steel barricades and police but there was no signage on why it wasn’t accessible.  There was also a language barrier so we couldn’t figure out why no one could go in the main part of the historic district. Petty big bummer since we only has one day there.

title: Cool spot but be cautious
text: A beautiful area to walk around with many amazing cathedrals and historic buildings. We toured a few churches. There were also a few people dressed up as characters that kids/fans might enjoy. Most spots seemed to be cash only but prices are low.Please use basic safety precautions. If you're taking pictures with your phone, keep a firm grip or have someone 'spot' you. You'll need small bills/dollar coins to enter most of the attractions. Make sure to store cash safely on your person. Avoid being out after dark. Even the locals do this so best to follow their lead.

title: Walk in the historic center
text: The historic center of Quito is simply charming! There are many ancient churches, which are worth visiting to see the details of the buildings. It is also worth walking to La Ronda, a gastronomic place where you can meet various restaurants and bars. The church museum is also worth visiting and at the end of the tour enjoy the craft beer.

title: Be vigilent of your surroundings
text: Our guide walked us down the Street of Seven Crosses and gave us a brief history of each of the seven churches along the street.  We then walked through Independence Square and then onto Plaza de San Francisco, and final a visit to Yumbos chocolate factory. The colonial architecture was amazing and everywhere was busy with locals and tourists, with many artists set up on the side of the streets selling paintings, knitted garments and much more. Plenty of police around on every corner so we felt relatively safe.

title: So crazy busy, hard to enjoy
text: While beautiful and historic, the beauty is ruined with graffiti and pan handlers. Many of the old buildings are covered in graffiti, the streets are extremely crowded, and everyone local (hotel staff and  taxi drivers) warned us to be very careful, and not to even display our cell phones because the theft is so bad. Even when in 2 different taxi the drivers told us to put our phone away while in the taxi. Very uncomfortable feeling.The constant worry ruined the beauty for me.  We spent about an hour and got out.  The traffic to get to old town is also crazy. Took nearly 20 minutes to get 6 blocks.

title: Not incredible.
text: The historic center of Quito, is the same as any historic center. Really nothing special. Watch out for the pickpoket.

title: Great Experience
text: Interesting history of the quito old town.We enjoyed walking the old town.Many nice places to eat and shop.worth visiting.

title: Safe and interesting
text: Really worth seeing, take a guide and listen about interesting history of Quito and also about nowadays situation, troubles, politics. You can manage almost all the major places in half day / day. Enjoy a cup of cocoa on your way

title: Exploring Quito through sights and tastes
text: Walking through Quito's Old Town is indeed a delightful experience. The cobblestone streets add a historic charm, transporting you back in time. The well-preserved buildings, with their colonial architecture and intricate details, showcase the city's rich history and cultural heritage. Around each corner, you’ll find quaint places to explore and enjoy a taste of their world-famous chocolate paired with delicious pastries. Quito will not only immerse you in visual sights but also in tasteful charm.

title: Voyage in South America
text: Beautiful, stylish city, colonial buildings, valuables left in the hotel, so walked freely, in the historical center lot of police. All very beautiful, lots of cafes

title: Beautiful walk for the family
text: For the weekend to escape a moment to tour the historic center is beautiful, the ease of the metro made us arrive in 7 minutes, the weather accompanied us and we could enjoy the churches and the round! Do not hesitate to visit if you have no plans for a weekend!!

title: One of the most beautiful in Latin America
text: In the great and chaotic capital there is this historic center that really left us speechless, both because the buildings, squares and various churches are preserved very well, but also for the atmosphere with colonial flavors and for the beautiful natural setting that surrounds it: in my opinion one of the most beautiful historic centers in a capital that I have seen in South and Central America! Traffic is a bit 'felt in the main arteries and at 2,850 m the smog is even heavier (never as much as in Cuenca!), but nevertheless inside are located purely pedestrian areas or limited traffic and so the problem I have not felt too much. The same goes for security: during the day it is heavily manned by police and police auxiliaries, so much so that I have never felt uncomfortable walking around (of course, as everywhere in the big cities of the world you always have to pay attention). Then I must say that Ecuadorians are fantastic people, kind and always available!

title: Very interesting visit
text: We spent 5 days mainly walking around the Old Town. The architecture is very interesting and historic. The chuiches magnificent. Great eating places with lots of interesting shops and chocolate tasting etc.Really worth spending time here

title: World Heritage Site
text: A world heritage site, the Historic Center of Quito preserves the colonial characteristics of the "master plan" of 1700: elongated and low houses, with patios, which extend onto the streets which, although on uneven terrain, reproduce whenever possible, a chessboard. And then the squares, with the central Plaza Major, and the churches and convents often examples of the Baroque school of Quito (Escuela Quitena), which brings together indigenous and European artistic traditions and which is renowned for having provided America's greatest contribution Spanish to universal art.I must admit that such beauty, however, did not move me deeply as it happened in other historic centers.

title: Beautiful historic center very well preserved and rich in history and art and excellent restaurants
text: Dedicate some time to this visit, better with an expert guide, so as not to miss the details of this beautiful historic center. Founded on Inca ruins, among colonial buildings and well-maintained squares, we were amazed at how clean and well-kept it was, the small balconies full of pots of geraniums were beautiful, but be careful of pickpockets and use caution in the evening. The interior of the churches is sumptuous, such as the Jesuit church of the Society of Jesus of Quito (Iglesia de la Compañía de Jesús) decorated with gold leaf ornaments, magnificent gilded ceilings, beautiful paintings and the gold-plated main altar. It took 160 years to complete Ecuador's most decorated church. The Church of San Francesco with its beautiful square and the convent are also worth visiting, as is the cathedral which overlooks the beautiful Plaza de la Independencia or the Gothic basilica, without forgetting the museums and excellent restaurants.

title: Must see.
text: Must visit old town. Is crowded compared to all other areas of Quito but very walkable. Plenty of police but still be aware of people around you.

title: Great on Sundays!
text: On a Sunday, the old town is closed to cars and you can have your fill of all the stalls in the streets and of course eat great food. Nice atmosphere and lots of music make it an experience.

title: It is an experience that feedbacks our knowledge.
text: It is very beautiful, you are filled with knowledge, an experience that you will like, recommended and if you want to take a tour with a professional tourist guide. Contact me 593 991350512.

title: Walking through the center during the day is a "must" if you visit Quito
text: The center is beautiful with many historic buildings and charming hidden patios. During the day the streets are bustling with people and the center is a giant market where you can find everything. After 6pm the center becomes empty, it is not dangerous but without the hustle and bustle it loses much of its charm.

title: Lots to look at...
text: It's just nice to walk around here... You just have to be careful, but there's no reason to feel unsafe.There is a lot to look at if you just walk around quietly. Lots of beautiful buildings, cafes and restaurants. Some of the restaurants have a roof terrace, with an absolutely fantastic view of Quito.Remember good shoes, a hat and sunscreen!We had our own guide, from the Andean travel company. Which is highly recommended.

title: Lovely Square
text: Sitting in the square by the San Francisco Church was a lovely way to spend an afternoon. Lots of street food on offer , the devil preaching ! Wedding parties and children having a great time. People watching with a coffee and there was a fantastic atmosphere

title: Fascinating City
text: We really enjoyed our stay in Quito.  The Old City is fascinating and there are so many good restaurants.  We had a private guide and recommend doing a guided tour. The churches are spectacular and and not to be missed.  Spend time ambling the streets to get a good feel for the City.

title: Quito, Carita de Dios.
text: Quito Old Town is the best Spanish Old Town in the Americas!It has overwhelming architecture, its churches are amazing.It has a natural outlook (El Panecillo hill) from where you can observe the city and its majestic Andes!Every time I go to Quito I continue to fall in love with this unique city surrounded by green beautiful mountains and snow capped peaks!

title: Such a special place
text: I am not surprised that Quito Old Town is in the list of the Unesco Heritage Sites. The colonial Spanish feel is all over the place with narrow streets and vast squares and the miriads of churches and convents. The walks up and down the three Main Streets (Quayaqil, Venezuela and Garcia Moreno) are full of little shops with interesting local artefacts and little restaurants and in the afternoon full of buskers and little stands of local craft and the music is coming from all over the places!Quito do not have the grandeur of other Colonial Spanish cities (like Havana or Old Panama) but I love the cosyness of the low raise buildings and the pastel colour of the houses.Certainly a wonderful discovery.

title: Well worth a walk around
text: Well worth a walk around, lovely old buildings, hole in the wall style shops, fabulous architecture. Do this walk through on te way to the square after the main church area. Particularly nice on a Sunday!

title: Cultural meld
text: Old Quito combines a lot of the style of both Baroque Spain and South America.  There are several plazas and wide pedestrian roads and every corner has an ornate church or palace on it.  Quito and Ecuador are not wealthy, but downtown Quito was vibrant and people were friendly, and although it was lively, it wasn't too crowded.  I got some sense that Quito could be dangerous, or at least that theft was a problem.  But I did not feel any sense of menace while I was in Old Quito (or other downtown areas).  There's a heavy police presence and the crowd seemed to be mostly tourists, school groups and downtown workers.

title: Music, dances, beautiful outfits in the Historic Center!!
text: Living the holidays and the expressive Viva Quito:)!!Touring this beautiful center in this important and special time!!Traditional music, dances, beautiful colorful costumes!!!Very happy atmosphere.

title: Worth a visit, it is the Old Town After all
text: You're in Quito and as with many cities the Old Town is a nice place to take in some history and enjoy the architecture and Quito is no different.  I was there during the daytime and with a guide who told me to flip my backpack to the front and carry it on my chest as well as moving and securing anything (wallet, etc.) in my pockets to my backpack where it was tightly under my control.  She is a local and a native so followed the advice and had no problems.  I felt safe in general but do appreciate that she was looking out for me.

title: Nice place
text: Our experience in old quito was nice even though was a bit hot.The church was the best part of the visit and worthwhile to see.

title: Beatiful and Historic with caveats
text: Quito's Centro Historico is considered one of the best preserved and largest colonial centers in the Americas, filled with buildings that are three hundred plus years old. Ornate churches seem to dot every corner.  So many of the buildings are simply works of art. Plazas located in the center are lively filled with locals, vendors, and tourists, at least during the day. There are a number of museums one can visit, along with numerous restaurants and stores.One caveat is generally speaking at night the old town is largely deserted and feels unsafe/seedy (other than the Plaza Independencia/presidential place area and La Ronda on weekends).  Another is even during day one must be somewhat careful, we were told by many people to mind our wallets, don't keep cellphones out and keep our wits in general. There is a sizeable homeless presence with some coming relatively close begging for money, however there is a large police presence during the day, overall it felt safe. In the end nothing happened to us and we enjoyed our visit.The old town is among the highlights of Quito, and should be visited, travel in groups if you can, don't wander alone at night, just be mindful of your surroundings and you should be fine.

title: A walk into history
text: The old city is amazingly preserved. Loved walking the streets, and seeing the churches, cathedrals, and courtyards. A must visit on your trip to Ecuador.

title: Full of character and safe for this solo traveller
text: First off, I never had any problems with pickpockets nor did I feel unsafe at anytime.  Like any other city, just be sensible ie don't wander alone at night in unlit areas, don't have an expensive camera just in your hand with no wrist or neck strap, in general don't make it easy for any potential thief.  I also found that many locals did know some English and were happy to help with directions for instance.It is a charming area to have a walk around, but be warned it is hilly and may be challenging if not yet accustomed to the high altitude as well.Plenty of stunning architecture to admire, especially inside the churches, also park areas with seats for resting up, lots of restaurants and cafes and souvenir shops.  Also many pet shops which I found was a theme throughout my trip of Ecuador, not sure why?!!

title: How to see Old City
text: We visited Quito while waiting to see the Galapagos islands.  The history of the old city was worth the visit.  We took one tour to see the country side and walked the city on our own.  I would highly recommend arranging a local tour so that you see more of the old city.  The tours outside the city involve an hour plus drive each way.  It was interesting but not a polished experience.  I would try the tours by locals if going again. The old city is just that—very old.  Streets are often narrow and often still paved with hand carved stones.  Be prepared to walk and to walk up hill.  It was a great stop over on our trip to the islands.We stayed at La Casana.  Hoetel and staff were great.

title: Don't Hesitate
text: Don't hesitate to visit this beautiful part of the city. In the daytime you are very safe, with normal considerations about keeping your valuables nearby. At night be sure to travel carefully with 2 or more together and stay away from dark alleys. It is a fun and interesting part o the city.

title: Take a few good masks!
text: Quito is huge noisy, busy, horrible pollution! Couldn’t wait to leave. Couple hours in old city was about churches, yet I enjoyed 5 minutes of a  couple men playing classical guitar on the street. For a country/city that depends on tourism I’m surprised how very few speak English. Means you hire a guide and their English isn’t as understandable as the price indicates, or their quality of information. Sorry, I was disappointed after months of planning.

title: Crazy traffic
text: Very very busy. My uber driver and I sat in traffic for at least an hour. Be mindful of when you go because traffic is insane. Other than having a delicious gelato, I didnt really enjoy my time here.

title: BE AWARE QUITO PICKPOCKETS
text: BE AWARE. Quito has gangs of pickpockets operating. We were targeted 4 times in a week. The main trick is to covertly spray you from behind with a mud solution that looks a bit like bird droppings. They then point it out and pull a handful of serviettes from their pocket and "help" you clean up. Whilst doing this they pick your pocket.Often others will join in to "help" BE AWARE do not let anyone help you. They were all well dressed middle aged and spoke good english.

title: Gorgeous!
text: This was my favorite area of Quito.  The buildings were amazing, the cathedrals stunning, and the overall look of the area was just gorgeous!  People are friendly and the streets lively, bustling during the days filled with vendors, locals and tourists.

title: Very Hilly
text: A very useful thing to know about Quito is that it is very hilly. When you plan a trip to this superb Andean capitol, knowing it is hilly will be key to your preparation. If it isn't, it should be

title: Old World Charm
text: Unesco world heritage site, for many good reasons. The architecture from colonial times is fascinating, especially the churches and monasteries. Streets have some moderate inclines, so with altitude, take your time strolling. Police/security presence is noticeable and gives a good sense of personal safety. Numerous street hawkers selling all types of goods, but they don't pester the tourists.

title: Nice busy area in the day
text: A very pleasant area to stroll around, well daytime at least. Many churches and old buildings to admire, Grand Plaza etc and particularly so one afternoon with a ceremony, dress uniforms on horseback and band in front of the Palacio de Carondelet. Less attractive at night as it is very quiet although we had no issues strolling from our local hotel to restaurants.

title: Well worth the walk
text: We followed the Lonely Planet walking tour. Starting at Grande Plaza we meandered backwards and forwards through the streets. Stopping off at churches, museums and coffee places. If you are in Quito don’t miss the opportunity to visit this area.

title: Great few day layover to Galapagos
text: We stayed for a total of 3 nights on either side of our amazing trip to Galapagos Islands and we were veryglad we did.  The old town area is very large with so many beautiful original Spanish Colonial buildingschurches and plazas with amazing history.   There are so many great restaurants and bars too with amazingviews.   The altitude may make you short of breath on walking up hill at first but the views at this altitude - 9000ft making it the highest capital in the world) are splendid.

title: A Must Visit Destination In Quito
text: You can't not visit the Historic District of Quito when visiting the City.The San Francisco Square, Historic Buildings, Museums, Historical Churches, Shopping, Markets, Restaurants and more, can all be found in the bustling and very popular part of Quito City.

title: Old town Quito
text: We walked around this quaint area without a guide because we only had a few hours. We discovered some beautiful monuments. It was starting to rain and we found a restaurant called Cafe Del Fraile in a building near the large square near the San Francisco monastery cathedral. We were able to get a taxi easily to and from the hotel. We later found out there was a hop on hop off bus that we could’ve gone on but we only had one day in the city.

title: Magical visit 2 the past
text: Immerse yourself in a pumping cityBreathtaking Architecture Kind interested polite peopleBeautiful intricate crafts

title: Quito, Ecuador
text: It was a beautiful city with old hotels and old churches. We stayed at the Friends Hotel, a beautiful, old, quaint hotel with the best staff and fantastic breakfasts! We had cake and coffee at the Artifice Cafeteria right down the street for another great experience.The down side was the thieves. Two of our group were robbed in the first two days we were there. Pickpockets and thieves are around every corner. Keep your purses closed and in your hand and wear your back packs on your front.

title: Huge Old Town
text: The old town of Quito is quite large and  is not like man other old towns.  While many of the old building have been perserved, there is a lot of business going on.  If in Quito then it is definitely a must do to spend time in the old town.  There are several beautiful churches and plazas to visit .  All can be seen in 4 hours.  There are many museums and here i would suggest to choose the ones which are of interest.  Real art / artisen goods are limited in the old town - most of the shops are basic goods for everyday use.  If looking for high quality goods then go to Casa Latina in the new town.

title: A wonderful experience
text: Quito is a big and beautiful city. To walk across its streets is a wonderful experience, you can find a lot of places where to eat traditional food or have some fun learning about the culture and historical buildings, but you will need some time if you want to discover all the wonders about this beautiful city. So I highly recommend to take a look at this city in your next holidays.

title: All Kinds of Shops
text: Old town has everything and all types of shops.  My favorites were the food shops selling rice, beans, spices, and the products needed for cooking every day.

title: Quito with Private Tour Operator.
text: Great Experience  of Old town Quito with Anywhere Ecuador. Our Guide Ricardo took us on the walking tour of all the landmarks explaining the history of Ecuador Independence.

title: Visit in Quito.
text: Nice place to walk and visit interested places.  Lot of nice small coffee houses and restaurants. Lot of historical old buildings to be explored.

title: Beautiful old town, but always felt on my guard
text: Our wonderful old hotel, up on a hill, was very close by the Old Town. So we were in the historic center many times on different occasions during our stay. I was aware of safety issues, so I tried to take the necessary precautions. I never did get 'victimized' but there was at least one occasion where I could see a 'set up' taking place involving two other people. This occurred close to the entrance of Calle La Ronda. The moment I sensed it, we simply walked away briskly towards more populated areas. And we got acquainted with some other tourists that had some unfortunate experiences. One was an attempted robbery at La Ronda, which started as a 'request for payment' just to walk on the street of La Ronda. Another couple we ran into were targeted for a scam in La Carolina park. In this situation, they spray you from behind, leading you to believe a bird pooped on your, then try to get 'touchy-feely' to help clean you up, all the while pick-pocketing. Based on some reviews, it sounds like the situation is worse in post-pandemic times. Also note that operating hours for business and tourist attractions are unreliable. Don't count on websites, travel books, or locals to give you accurate information. We could not even get accurate info from the street police, or the tourist info center. For example, the 'changing of the guard' advertised in travel books is no longer taking place since the pandemic. And yet many locals told us that it was. We only knew for sure by showing up at the designated time, and nothing was happening. Note that this was my experience in the summer of 2022.

title: Too bad that it gets very desolate after 6 pm
text: The churches are beautiful.  Had coffee and bunuelos at Hotel Patio Andaluz-- great coffee as well as service.

title: Beautiful but dangerous
text: Main plaza is safe as it's packing with cops, the rest of the Centro historico is dangerous full of prostitute's, muggers and undesirables hanging around , which is a shame as the place has some real nice old buildings to visit, Ecuador was once one if the safer countries in the region but sadly not anymore as the inequality gap has has stretched considerably.

title: Very fun time.
text: Very nice time.  The architecture was very interesting. The square was vibrant.  The restaurants were great.  They say that you haven't seen Quito until you've been to the Old Town,  and this seems to be correct.

title: The city took me back in time
text: The city takes you back in time because it has so much history, it is also one of a kind UNESCO World Heritage Site. One can explore the city on foot, see new galleries, and smell amazing freshly baked goods on each corner. There are some really interesting museums, shops and churches. The most people-friendly place and safe to explore the city.

title: Great experience
text: This place has a rich history and you can feel it while taking a tour around the historic district. I really enjoyed exploring it!

title: Very fun and safe place to walk and explore
text: There are pickpockets, but most people are friendly and open for you to browse and explore,  Most people will only speak Spanish though.

title: Nice for exploring around
text: There is a lot of history here so it would be worth it to take a tour around the historic district. The whole historic district was named a UNESCO World Heritage Site so it's definitely worth going to.

title: Old Town
text: Wasn't super impressed, parts were crazy packed and people going all directions. We've been to Chinatown in Bangkok on a weekend. Views from on top of the churches were amazing. We're going to try a walking guided tour, was a bit hard solo. Churches were well worth the effort but hindsight I would Uber to each spot maybe.

title: Well preserved Spanish colonial center
text: Quito's compact colonial center is one of the best preserved in Latin America.  Worth a stroll and definitely worth entering some of the amazing churches and other colonial buildings.  Nothing 5 star here but a really nice escape from the rest of the city.

title: The old town is a must when visit Quito
text: The old town is very historic and filled with buildings from 300 to 500 years old.  You can walk the town.   Traffic is bad in this city,

title: Sprawling but worth it
text: Quito’s 600 year old old town is sprawling. Mosh mash of beautifully restored churches, palace, Monastery, as well as electronics shops, souvenirs, etc. Follow orange flowers painted in asphalt to reach center of town. Affordable lunches all over, even with the best views.

title: Interesting
text: A tremendous amount of history within the blocks of the square - much to see and do.  Felt safe due to the number of police in the area.  (We were there during numerous rallies/protests)

title: Beauty and history at the doorstep of the Andes.
text: Somewhat free from the restrictions imposed by the COVID pandemic, and initially scheduled for April of 2020, my wife and I finally embarked on our South American trip to Quito and the Galapagos Islands, in the beautiful country of Ecuador.Quito is a city worth seeing but we highly recommend using a guide. It will cost you about 100$ per person (if you are a couple), per day, but it will save time and frustration trying to make your way from one point of interest to another. We were immensely satisfied with the services of our guide from “Tours by Local”. Do remember that Quito is a high-altitude city of close to 2850 meters (9350ft.), so take it slow and pace yourselves. For some, higher elevations may mean greater difficulty in breathing, sleeping, headaches, or a combination of all. From old Spanish colonial and religious sites, to overseeing the city from the mountain top, tasting your way through culinary delights, and above all people that are friendly and kind, Quito has much to offer for the quasi “off-beaten-path” traveler. As Quito is no different from any other city of importance throughout the world, vigilance is recommended.  Some quarters should not be walked alone (as a couple or individually) after dark. Otherwise, have an experience of a life time.

title: Worth an Afternoon's Exploration
text: This UNESCO World Heritage Site showcases 16th century Spanish culture with many churches, the seat of government, narrow walkways, and people of different cultures in a very walkable area.

title: COLONIAL ARCHITECTURE
text: A most interesting walking tour amidst Spanish colonial architecture. I've seen it in other countries. I never get tired of being in the midst of it. Well worth the time spent here.

title: Beautifully preserved old colonial town
text: I have been to a ton of different cities colonized by the Spanish that lead to a similar type of architecture more or less - Bogotá, Cartagena, Lima, Arequipa, Havana, San Juan, Granada, León, Ciudad de Panamá, Ciudad de México, Mérida, etc.. I can confidently say that Quito has some of the most extensive and well preserved networks of buildings. There's a reason it's a UNESCO World Heritage Site! I would recommend spending weekdays here as that's when it's most busy - on the weekends it gets kind of desolate and there aren't a lot of businesses open.

title: So many interesting places to see
text: A world heritage city with so many unique buildings and squares to visit. The busy traffics and polluted air was an issue but Quito is a great place to admire the colonial architecture and churches.

title: A highly recommended experience of Old Town
text: I really enjoyed the experience of Old Town, Quito.  The architecture, the cobble streets, the history, food, culture... all of it was very enjoyable.  We had a guided walking tour that lasted about 3+ hours.  Our guide was incredibly knowledgable and took us to some great places.  Most people are very friendly and always smiled at me when I smiled at them.  Other than a few tasteless men cat-calling (whom I ignored), I didn't experience any negative interactions.  I was carrying around my large, Canon SLR, which did make me a little uneasy, but I was with 5 other people (including my husband).  The guide said that the pandemic has made poverty and crime worse in the city and that pick-pocketing can happen if you leave your wallet or cell-phone exposed.  He said that as long as we were keeping our valuables secure, we should be okay.  There were a couple areas he did call out as being unsafe during the night and so we should avoid.  Right now (Jan/Feb 2022), for anyone considering a visit soon, they do require you to show your vaccination card to enter many places so one good recommendation our guide gave was to take a photo of your important documents, your ID, passport, vaccination card, and to never carry them on you in case you do become a victim of theft or pick-pocketing.

title: Definitely something to experience
text: Really a great place for architecture and history. We had a guide helped immensely. We visited numerous place. Be prepared for a work out as the streets are quite hilly

title: Spectacular City
text: We had a lovely tour with our guide around all the sights and sounds. The parade  at the Basilica was amazing.

title: A fun day at the Artisanal Market
text: We had a free day waiting for our covid test results before flying to the Galapagos Islands for a (fantastic!) cruise on LaPinta.  We walked there from the JWMarriott (very nice) & shopped for an hour or so, not even covering a fraction of the market (we had 3 young boys in our group).  There were some beautiful items for sale at very reasonable prices.  We felt very safe walking as a group of 8 back & forth from the hotel.

title: Loved it!
text: I love Quito and do love the old city area of Quito. Lots of history and beautiful architecture. Whenever I visit Quito, I always stay in the old district.

title: Dirty and unsafe
text: Be prepared for lots of graffiti, stray dogs, homeless people and poop on the sidewalks.Also, everything is basically closed all the time.And apparently it’s not safe to go there at night.I guess it’s the best part of Quito but not comparable to other cities and nothing spectacular.

title: Historic city
text: We had a great guide in Washington sierra. Hope you are able to meet him, we found his contact info on a forum in trip advisor. He is a retired Spanish teacher, drives taxi, a great tourist guide who speaks English. He took us all around Quito. We enjoyed the views and architecture. We had good weather but it fogs up on and off. Definitely check out the domes and church

title: My favorite party of Quito
text: To be honest I didn't think I was going to like this area as much as I did.  So many things to see, so much going on.  I highly recommend a guide to give you the most information.

title: Great place to stay and visit all the historical sites
text: We spent 4 nights at the Casa Montero right across the street from the Santo Domingo church and had a wonderful experience. We were able to walk to most anywhere in Old Town including La Ronda street where the food was great, Presidential Square, San Francisco Church, National Biscilica, among many other amazing sites. It was a busy time and we enjoyed the dancing and music from our room due to the celebration activities leading up to today which is the Foundation of Quito which is a local holiday but there was a lot of pre-celebrations each night.

title: Historical Muslim (moorish) and Spanish architecture
text: A nice stroll through the old town displaying Muslim and Spanish architecture, especially windows and gates/doors of the buildings. Cobble streets add to the environment. Wish it would be considered safe walking alone. Felt a certain tense even with the guide. Armed guards at stores do not provide relief
